title: linaria tenuis var. ascalonica
intro: Linaria tenuis var. ascalonica, also known as Toadflax, is a flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Toadflax include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues. Toadflax can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its flowers and leaves can be used in herbal preparations, such as salves and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
